commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Missing aria-labels for humidity and wind speed data points.
issues.fix: Add aria-label attributes to the metric text containers to describe their purpose for screen readers.

issues.description: The forecast mapping assumes data presence; using default values (N/A, 0) is good, but the rendering logic for icons is limited.
issues.fix: Ensure the logic for matching icons is robust or pass icon components directly via props if possible.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, clean, and follows the design system primitives effectively. The visual hierarchy is clear and the responsive layout for the forecast strip is well-handled; minor accessibility additions would make it perfect.

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates solid use of design tokens and proper data-driven rendering, but has some layout concerns: the forecast strip may overflow on smaller screens without responsive wrapping, and the hardcoded emoji fallback logic for weather conditions should be data-driven. Polish is slightly lacking with no hover/focus states or transitions.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Grid cells are treated as click targets but are implemented as <div> elements without button roles, keyboard support (onKeyDown), or tabindex, making them inaccessible to screen readers and keyboard users.
issues.fix: Convert grid cells to <button> elements or add role='button', tabIndex={0}, and an onKeyDown handler that triggers the onClick logic.

issues.description: The grid lacks visual responsiveness for smaller screens; 18 columns will overflow on standard laptops without a container scroll or responsive layout adjustment.
issues.fix: Wrap the grid in an overflow-x-auto div to ensure it doesn't break the layout on narrower viewports.

issues.description: The search functionality visually dims non-matching elements, but the interaction feels static; there is no hover effect on elements to indicate they are interactive.
issues.fix: Add a `:hover` style (e.g., box-shadow or scale transform) to the grid cells to provide immediate feedback.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and uses the provided design tokens effectively for a clean look. It achieves all functional requirements, though it needs accessibility enhancements for the custom grid cells to be considered production-ready.

evaluation.critique: The component successfully uses CSS Grid with 18 columns and design tokens throughout, but has layout issues: the grid doesn't handle non-matching elements well (opacity masking creates visual clutter), and the periodic table spacing is minimal. The detail panel appears below all elements rather than in a cleaner position. Interactive polish is decent with opacity transitions, but lacks hover states on element cells and focus states on the search input.

commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Forecast array fields are optional in the contract and some rendering uses fallbacks, but the component doesn't enforce that it always shows 5 days from the provided data beyond padding missing items with '—'. This is fine, but it may mask incomplete data without signaling it clearly.
issues.fix: Optionally render an explicit 'N/A' or dimmed style for missing day/icon/high/low entries to better communicate missing data.

issues.description: The forecast strip uses an outer rounded container with border, but inner vertical separators are only applied via borderLeft and spacing can feel uneven for the first and last columns due to asymmetric padding logic (idx === 0 and idx === 4).
issues.fix: Apply consistent horizontal padding for all cells, and instead control edge spacing with container padding or a uniform cell padding approach.

issues.description: There are no interactive elements, hover/focus states, keyboard behavior, or loading/validation states. While a weather card is mostly static, the requirement set for interactivity polish is unmet.
issues.fix: If the component is intended to be interactive (e.g., unit toggle, city selection, refresh), add accessible buttons/controls with proper focus and hover states. If it must remain static, explicitly ensure non-interactive semantics are correct (e.g., no misleading clickable UI).

issues.description: The weather icon is rendered as a span with aria-hidden and the surrounding text includes the condition; however, the icon itself isn't given an accessible name if the condition text is short/empty. Also, using emoji for meaning is okay, but relying on string matching to choose an emoji could lead to mismatches with the provided condition text.
issues.fix: If props.condition can be empty or unexpected, ensure there is always a meaningful accessible label (e.g., include a visually hidden description). Optionally allow the caller to pass an explicit icon in props rather than inferring.

issues.description: Inline style objects and repeated inline computations (e.g., icon/condition mapping IIFE inside render) may slightly reduce readability and can cause unnecessary recalculations (though not severe).
issues.fix: Extract the condition-to-emoji mapping and forecast cell styling/padding logic into small helper functions or memoized values for clarity and maintainability.

evaluations.critique: The component covers the requested weather card content well: prominent current temperature, condition with an icon, humidity and wind panels, and a bottom 5-day forecast strip using only prop-driven data. The layout has a clear hierarchy and pleasant spacing with consistent card/strip styling. Interactivity is essentially absent (static card), and while that may be acceptable, the implementation lacks any interactive polish or accessible controls if the wider UI expects them.

evaluation.critique: The component has excellent data handling with all weather information correctly sourced from props and no hardcoded weather data. Layout is responsive with proper flex usage and good spacing. Main weaknesses: some spacing uses raw pixel values (24, 8, 6, 4) instead of design tokens, and missing interactive polish like hover/focus states on the forecast items or weather metric boxes.

violations: Hardcoded string "Tokyo" found — should be a prop default
Hardcoded string "Sunny" found — should be a prop default

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.
